title: Incorrect result of getting data from a spatial index on column with SRID
description:
The result is empty when MySQL gets data from a spatial index containing a column with SRID attributes.
How to repeat:
- 1. create table

CREATE TABLE `a` (
  `id` int  auto_increment NOT NULL,
  `shape` geometry not null /*!80003 SRID 0 */,
  PRIMARY KEY (`id`),
  SPATIAL KEY (`shape`)
) ENGINE=InnoDB;

- 2. add data

insert a values (2, point(1,100));

- 3. query data from the spatial index is empty
select id from a force index (shape) where id > 0;
Empty set (0.00 sec)

- 4. query data from the primary key is correct

select id from a force index (primary) where id > 0;
+----+
| id |
+----+
|  2 |
+----+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
bug_features_seed:
CREATE TABLE `b` (
`id` int auto_increment NOT NULL,
`shape` geometry not null,
`name` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
PRIMARY KEY (`id`),
SPATIAL KEY (`shape`)
);
INSERT INTO b (shape, name) VALUES (point(1, 100), 'Point A');
INSERT INTO b (shape, name) VALUES (point(2, 200), 'Point B');
SELECT id FROM b WHERE ST_Contains(shape, point(1, 100));
SELECT id FROM b WHERE id IS NULL;
key_bug_features: Querying spatial index with SRID results in empty set
